proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_013 is associated with lower objective_response rate (binary marker, expected sign negative).
feature_008 is associated with higher objective_response rate (binary marker/treatment, expected sign positive).
feature_021 is associated with lower objective_response rate.
feature_015 is associated with lower objective_response rate.
feature_027 is associated with lower objective_response rate.
feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004 each have no marginal association with objective_response.
feature_030 is constant across the cohort and provides no information.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_013')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 15.4% when feature_013=1 vs 34.6% when feature_013=0; chi-square p<1e-300.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 36.1% when feature_008=1 vs 15.9% when feature_008=0; chi-square p<1e-300.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_021')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 15.0% when feature_021=1 vs 25.0% when feature_021=0; chi-square p=5.4e-56.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_015')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 16.0% when feature_015=1 vs 26.0% when feature_015=0; chi-square p=1.9e-98.

analyses.code: df.groupby('feature_027')['objective_response'].mean(); chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is 17.6% when feature_027=1 vs 24.2% when feature_027=0; chi-square p=3.0e-9.

analyses.code: for c in [feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004]: chi2_contingency
analyses.result_summary: All seven features have p>=0.30 for chi-square tests against objective_response; no marginal association detected. feature_006 p=0.87, feature_005 p=0.30, feature_023 p=0.47, feature_011 p=0.91, feature_017 p=0.89, feature_019 p=0.60, feature_004 p=0.88.

analyses.code: df['feature_030'].nunique()
analyses.result_summary: feature_030 has only one value (0) across all 50000 rows. Confirmed uninformative; dropped from subsequent analyses.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous feature_022 (range 0.12-3622, highly skewed) is negatively associated with objective_response (e.g., higher values predict lower response).
Continuous feature_020 is negatively associated with objective_response.
Continuous feature_024 is negatively associated with objective_response.
Multi-level feature_001 (0/1/2) shows monotonically decreasing objective_response across levels.
feature_010 (5-level ordinal) shows no association with objective_response.
feature_002 is positively associated with objective_response.
feature_016 (age-like, range 30-90) is not associated with objective_response.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_022 split by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_022 is 28.2 in responders vs 47.2 in non-responders (Welch t-test p=1.5e-137). Strongly negatively associated.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_020 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_020 is 3.50 in responders vs 3.95 in non-responders (p=6.8e-31).

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_024 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_024 is 5.69 in responders vs 6.23 in non-responders (p=9.3e-9).

analyses.code: Cross-tab and ordinal logistic on feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Response rate by feature_001: 28.2% (level 0), 22.8% (level 1), 18.2% (level 2). Logistic slope -0.285 per level, p=9.5e-73. Monotonic decreasing as hypothesized.

analyses.code: chi2_contingency on feature_010 vs response
analyses.result_summary: Response rate is essentially flat across feature_010 levels 6-10 (24.4%, 23.7%, 24.2%, 24.2%, 24.2%); chi-square p=0.77.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_002 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_002 is 3.82 in responders vs 3.80 in non-responders (p=3.6e-6). Small positive association.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_016 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_016 is 65.0 in responders vs 65.0 in non-responders (p=0.82). No association.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After controlling for all other features in a multivariable logistic regression, feature_008 retains a strong positive coefficient (>1 logit) and feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 retain strong negative coefficients.
feature_022 retains a small but significant negative coefficient in the multivariable model.

Several features (feature_018, feature_026, feature_028, feature_007, feature_032, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_011, feature_023, feature_006, feature_005) lose any significance in the multivariable model, suggesting they are noise or fully mediated.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, sm.add_constant(df[all_feats])).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit coefficients: feature_008=+1.20 (z=52.6), feature_013=-1.13 (z=-45.4), feature_015=-0.69 (z=-22.1), feature_021=-0.74 (z=-16.9), feature_027=-0.43 (z=-6.0). All five have p<1e-8; signs as predicted.

analyses.code: (same model)
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 coefficient = -0.0010 per unit (z=-5.5, p=3e-8) in the full multivariable model. Significant but small per-unit; large because of wide range.

analyses.code: (same model)
analyses.result_summary: Confirmed: feature_018, feature_026, feature_028, feature_007, feature_032, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_011, feature_023, feature_006, feature_005, feature_010, feature_016, feature_031 all have p>0.05 in the multivariable model. feature_002 (p=2e-8), feature_020 (p<1e-30), and feature_024 (p<1e-7) remain significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 is a treatment whose effect on objective_response is heterogeneous: it is significantly modified (interaction) by feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 with negative interaction sign in each case (the marker suppresses the treatment benefit).
feature_008 effect is also negatively modified by continuous feature_022 (higher feature_022 reduces the treatment benefit).

Among other binary features with no main effect (feature_006, feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004), none show a strong (LR>=10) interaction with any other feature, ruling them out as additional treatments.

analyses.code: for f in features: LR test of feature_008 + f vs feature_008 + f + feature_008:f
analyses.result_summary: Interaction LR tests for feature_008 with each binary marker: feature_013 LR=1885 p<<1e-300 (beta_int=-1.98); feature_015 LR=427 p<<1e-300 (beta_int=-1.24); feature_021 LR=188 p<1e-42 (beta_int=-1.14); feature_027 LR=85 p=2.6e-20 (beta_int=-1.28). All four interactions are negative as hypothesized.

analyses.code: LR test feature_008 + feature_022 vs feature_008 + feature_022 + feature_008:feature_022
analyses.result_summary: Interaction beta = -0.0087 per unit feature_022; LR=348 p<1e-77. Higher feature_022 substantially reduces treatment benefit.

analyses.code: LR interaction screen for each candidate against all other features
analyses.result_summary: Best interaction p-values: feature_006 x feature_019 LR=6.7 p=0.010; feature_005 x feature_027 LR=3.9 p=0.048; feature_023 x feature_021 LR=4.6 p=0.032; feature_011 x feature_007 LR=5.2 p=0.023; feature_017 x feature_028 LR=4.7 p=0.031; feature_019 x feature_006 LR=6.7 p=0.010; feature_004 x feature_010 LR=4.5 p=0.033. None exceed LR=10. Most likely false positives at this scale (~32 tests per feature).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the joint model with feature_008, feature_013, feature_021, feature_015, feature_027, and the four feature_008-by-marker interactions, the four interactions are independently significant and negative.

The main effects of feature_013, feature_015, feature_021, feature_027 in patients without feature_008 (control arm) are small or null because they primarily act by suppressing treatment benefit, not by harming untreated patients.

analyses.result_summary: Joint model: feature_008 main = +2.58 (z=70). All four interaction terms strongly negative and significant: f008xf013=-2.20 (z=-45), f008xf021=-1.52 (z=-17), f008xf015=-1.61 (z=-25), f008xf027=-1.65 (z=-11). Hypothesis confirmed.

analyses.code: (same model)
analyses.result_summary: Marker main effects in control arm (feature_008=0): f013=-0.14 (z=-4.4, p<0.001), f021=-0.10 (z=-1.9, p=0.06), f015=-0.02 (z=-0.6, p=0.56), f027=+0.18 (z=+2.2, p=0.03). Effects on untreated patients are small (between -0.14 and +0.18 logits) compared with the suppression of the +2.58 treatment effect, supporting the hypothesis: these markers act primarily as treatment-effect modifiers, not as strong main prognostic factors in untreated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0 ('clean' subgroup), feature_008 raises objective_response from ~17% to ~80% (absolute difference > 0.5).
In patients with any of feature_013=1, feature_021=1, feature_015=1, feature_027=1, the feature_008 treatment effect is essentially null (absolute difference within +/- 0.03).
Within the clean subgroup, no other feature significantly modifies the feature_008 treatment effect (no LR interaction p<0.01).

analyses.code: df[clean_mask].groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Clean subgroup (n=15681): feature_008=1 response rate = 79.8% (n=6325); feature_008=0 = 17.2% (n=9356). ARR=+62.6 percentage points; NNT=1.6. Hypothesis strongly confirmed.

analyses.code: df[~clean_mask].groupby('feature_008')['objective_response'].mean()
analyses.result_summary: Unclean subgroup (n=34319): feature_008=1 response rate = 16.1% (n=13751); feature_008=0 = 15.3% (n=20568). ARR=+0.8 percentage points; NNT=130. Effect essentially null as hypothesized.

analyses.code: Within clean subgroup, LR interaction screen of feature_008 with each other feature
analyses.result_summary: Best modifiers within clean subgroup: feature_005 LR=8.3 p=0.004 (beta_int=-0.25), feature_017 LR=4.4 p=0.035, feature_026 LR=3.6 p=0.058, feature_001 LR=3.3 p=0.069. With Bonferroni correction across ~28 tests (alpha=0.0018), none survive. Hypothesis supported.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the unclean subgroup, no feature is a meaningful effect modifier that 'rescues' the feature_008 treatment effect.
Among the 17 continuous features, only feature_022 has a strong (LR>50) treatment-effect interaction with feature_008.

analyses.code: Within unclean subgroup, LR interaction screen of feature_008 with each other feature
analyses.result_summary: Top within-unclean modifiers: feature_005 LR=8.3 p=0.004 (beta_int=+0.18 implying small rescue), feature_025 LR=3.4 p=0.064. None survive Bonferroni correction; subgroup-level treatment effect remains essentially zero.

analyses.code: LR interaction tests for feature_008 x each continuous feature
analyses.result_summary: feature_022 LR=348 p<1e-77 (beta_int=-0.0087); feature_025 LR=4.4 p=0.035 (only other significant). All other continuous features p>0.05. feature_022 is the lone strong continuous modifier. (Notably, within the clean subgroup the feature_022 interaction disappears, so the marker effect is captured by f013/021/015/027.)

proposed_hypotheses.text: A T-learner heterogeneous treatment effect (HTE) decision tree of depth 3 will recover feature_013, feature_015, feature_021 as the three top splitting variables, recapitulating the 'clean' subgroup.

analyses.code: T-learner: LogisticRegression on T=1 and T=0 separately; estimate ITE; fit DecisionTreeRegressor(max_depth=3) on ITE
analyses.result_summary: Top splits in tree: root=feature_013 (<=0.5 vs >0.5); within feature_013<=0.5: feature_015 then feature_021. Largest ITE leaf = 0.55 (feature_013=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_021=0). Lowest ITE leaves = -0.13 to -0.09 in feature_013=1 / feature_015=1 / feature_001>=1 subgroup. Tree confirms the 'clean' subgroup definition; feature_027 is too rare (3% prevalence) to enter at depth 3 but joins the definition by interaction model.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_008 was assigned independently of all other measured features in the cohort (consistent with randomized assignment), as evidenced by all standardized mean differences |SMD| < 0.05.

analyses.code: for each feature: SMD = (mean(f008=1) - mean(f008=0)) / sd(feature)
analyses.result_summary: All 30 baseline features have |SMD| < 0.02 between feature_008=1 and feature_008=0 groups (largest |SMD| = 0.019 for feature_017). This is consistent with randomized assignment of feature_008 and rules out unmeasured confounding as the explanation for the +20 percentage-point main effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_001 (3-level) is a prognostic factor (lower response at higher levels) but is NOT a significant modifier of the absolute feature_008 treatment effect.
feature_010 (5-level) is neither prognostic for response nor a modifier of the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Stratified response rates by feature_001 within each arm; LR interaction of feature_008 x feature_001
analyses.result_summary: Arm-stratified rates: feature_008=0: f001=0 19.9%, f001=1 14.7%, f001=2 10.3%; feature_008=1: f001=0 40.6%, f001=1 34.8%, f001=2 30.1%. Absolute treatment effect: 20.7%, 20.1%, 19.8% across the three levels - essentially equal. LR interaction p=0.002 in additive model (multiplicative scale captures small variation), but on the absolute risk-difference scale the effect modification is negligible. Hypothesis confirmed: prognostic but not predictive.

analyses.code: Cross-tab feature_010 by response within each arm
analyses.result_summary: Response rates across feature_010 levels 6-10 essentially flat in each arm. Overall chi-square p=0.77. Confirmed: feature_010 has no role in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_022 is prognostic for objective_response within both treated and untreated arms (negative slope), consistent with a tumor-burden marker.
feature_020 is independently prognostic for objective_response in both arms.
feature_002 has a small positive prognostic effect, mostly in the untreated arm.

analyses.code: Within feature_008==1 and feature_008==0 separately, t-test for feature_022 by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Untreated arm: mean feature_022 36 (R+) vs 44 (R-), p=1.4e-12. Treated arm: mean 23 (R+) vs 53 (R-), p=4.6e-148. Strongly negative association in both arms; effect is much larger in treated arm, consistent with the f008 x f022 interaction.

analyses.code: ttest_ind feature_020 by response within each arm
analyses.result_summary: Untreated arm: 3.39 (R+) vs 3.92 (R-), p=9.1e-20. Treated arm: 3.57 (R+) vs 4.01 (R-), p=2.3e-15. Independently prognostic in both arms.

analyses.code: ttest_ind feature_002 by response within each arm
analyses.result_summary: Untreated arm: 3.83 (R+) vs 3.80 (R-), p=2.5e-6. Treated arm: difference not significant. Small effect concentrated in untreated patients.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Even within the 'clean' subgroup, the prognostic gradient by feature_001 persists (lower response at higher feature_001 even with treatment) - i.e., feature_001 is prognostic but not predictive.
Within the clean subgroup, feature_022 is no longer a meaningful effect modifier of feature_008.

analyses.code: Stratified subgroup means within clean by feature_001 and feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Clean & f001=0: f008=1 response 85.9% vs f008=0 21.3% (ARR=+64.6). Clean & f001=2: f008=1 response 70.9% vs f008=0 11.9% (ARR=+59.0). Treatment effect remains huge across all feature_001 levels within the clean subgroup; the prognostic gradient on the control arm is confirmed but does not erase treatment benefit.

analyses.code: Within clean subgroup, response by feature_022 quintile and feature_008
analyses.result_summary: Within clean subgroup, feature_008 ARR by feature_022 quintile (Q1-Q5): +0.640, +0.630, +0.598, +0.647, +0.610. Effect modification on the absolute scale is essentially zero. The original feature_008 x feature_022 interaction observed marginally is fully captured by the four binary markers - feature_022 is correlated with the markers but adds no independent modification once those are conditioned on.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Adding the four feature_008 x marker interactions to a full additive logistic model meaningfully improves predictive AUC (>=0.01 absolute gain).
feature_008 alone, within the clean subgroup, has AUC > 0.75 for predicting objective_response.

analyses.code: LogisticRegression with vs without four interactions; in-sample AUC
analyses.result_summary: Full additive model AUC = 0.7214; with four feature_008 x {f013, f021, f015, f027} interactions added AUC = 0.7333. Gain of +0.012 absolute - meaningful and confirms the interactions carry independent predictive information.

analyses.code: roc_auc_score(y_clean, feature_008_clean)
analyses.result_summary: Within clean subgroup (n=15681), feature_008 alone yields AUC = 0.808. Treatment status is the dominant determinant of response in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_005, feature_023, feature_011, feature_017, feature_019, feature_004, feature_006 are all noise covariates (no main effect, no interaction with feature_008, no interaction with each other beyond chance).

analyses.code: All-pairs interaction screen among the 'no-main-effect' binary features; comparison with feature_008 in arm-stratified analyses
analyses.result_summary: No interaction among these features achieves LR>10. Within each feature_008 arm, none of these binary features shows a chi-square p<0.001 association with response (max chi-square p was 0.07 for feature_005 in the unclean subgroup). Conclusion: these features are uninformative covariates (consistent with being included in the EHR feature panel but not biologically relevant to this treatment).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Best-supported subgroup hypothesis: feature_008 (treatment) increases objective_response by approximately 60-65 percentage points specifically in patients with feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0; in patients with any of these markers positive, the treatment effect is essentially null.

analyses.code: Final stratified analysis with the joint subgroup definition; chi-square test of f008 within clean subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Final subgroup: feature_013=0 AND feature_021=0 AND feature_015=0 AND feature_027=0 (n=15681; 31.4% of cohort). Within this subgroup: f008=1 response 79.8% vs f008=0 17.2% (ARR=+62.6 pp; chi2 p<<1e-300; OR=18.9). Outside this subgroup (n=34319): f008=1 response 16.1% vs f008=0 15.3% (ARR=+0.8 pp; chi2 p=0.07; OR=1.06). The four-marker subgroup definition is necessary and sufficient: each of the four markers individually abolishes the treatment effect (each diff in 'X-only' subgroup within +/-0.025), and within the clean subgroup no other variable adds predictive value.
